review_reason: Phase 6 DenselyOrdered proof on BidirectionalQuotient from DN has a mathematical gap: constrained Lindenbaum achieves one-sided strictness (a<c<=b or a<=c<b) but not both simultaneously. The research sketch (research-012 Section 3.3) handwaved this step. See handoffs/phase-6-blocker-20260309.md for detailed analysis and 5 potential approaches.

artifacts.summary: Detailed analysis of Phase 6 mathematical blocker with 5 potential approaches

partial_progress.details: Phase 6 blocked: DenselyOrdered on BidirectionalQuotient requires showing both not(c<=a) and not(b<=c) for intermediate c. Each direction proven separately (constrained_forward_above, constrained_forward_below) but combining them requires a seed not subset of either a.world or b.world.

errors.message: Cannot simultaneously force a < c and c < b in BidirectionalQuotient using single distinguishing formula. G(psi) and neg(psi) are contradictory (T-axiom). Two-formula approach requires non-trivial consistency proof.
errors.recommendation: Review mathematical argument. Consider approaches: (1) two-formula seed with consistency proof, (2) adjacency contradiction, (3) irreflexive setting, (4) direct Q-embedding.

completion_data.completion_summary: Proved constrained Lindenbaum lemmas for one-sided strictness (constrained_forward_above: a<c<=b, constrained_forward_below: a<=c<b). Phase 6 DenselyOrdered instance blocked by inability to achieve both strictness conditions simultaneously.
